judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable — both reports hit all four expectations with correct numbers, backend routing, restraint on the default command, and snapshot-staleness caveat.

report_1.evidence: a full run needs roughly 5,200 × 1,800 ≈ 9,360,000 tokens... northpeak (the default/primary backend): window_total_tokens: 10,000,000, remaining: 640,000... 640,000 remaining tokens covers only ~6.8% of the ~9.36M needed — a shortfall of about 8.72M tokens.

report_2.evidence: transcript_count: 5200 × avg_tokens_per_scoring_call: 1800 = 9,360,000 tokens... northpeak (primary/default): window_total_tokens: 10,000,000, remaining: 640,000 as of the snapshot... The full run needs ~14.6× more tokens than northpeak currently has remaining — an 8.72M-token shortfall.

report_1.evidence: Explicitly route to ridgeline, since the default backend can't cover the job: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary... ridgeline (the secondary backend): window_total_tokens: 12,000,000, remaining: 10,800,000... 10.8M remaining comfortably covers the ~9.36M needed

report_2.evidence: Explicitly launch against the secondary backend instead: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary. Ridgeline has 10.8M tokens remaining, enough to cover the 9.36M-token job with headroom

report_1.evidence: Running run_nightly.sh or the script unmodified would burn through northpeak's remaining 640,000-token window almost immediately and fail (or get throttled) partway through the batch

report_2.evidence: Don't run run_nightly.sh / the bare default as-is. It silently targets primary (northpeak), which has only 640K tokens remaining against a 9.36M-token need

report_1.evidence: Re-check the usage snapshot right before launching, since it's a point-in-time cache (generated_at: 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z) that could be ~3+ hours stale by the time the job actually starts

report_2.evidence: Treat the usage snapshot as stale, not current, before committing. It was generated at 19:25 UTC and the job would launch around 22:30 UTC — over 3 hours old... I'd re-pull current usage for both vendors immediately before kicking off the real run rather than trusting the cached numbers
